title: Fix a typo in batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap
body:
The current implementation can cause `reproj_mask` to be undefined in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap()` if `max_reproj_error = None` causing the following: 

```
if masks is not None and reproj_mask is not None:
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'reproj_mask' referenced before assignment
``` 

Initializing `reproj_mask` beforehand fix that error.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -51,6 +51,8 @@ def batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap(
     assert len(points3d) == P
     assert image_size.shape[0] == 2
 
+    reproj_mask = None
+
     if max_reproj_error is not None:
         projected_points_2d, projected_points_cam = project_3D_points_np(points3d, extrinsics, intrinsics)
         projected_diff = np.linalg.norm(projected_points_2d - tracks, axis=-1)

steps.operation: Initialize `reproj_mask` to `None` at the start of `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap` so it is always defined regardless of `max_reproj_error`
steps.reason: When `max_reproj_error` is `None`, the existing code path skips the block that assigns `reproj_mask`, but later logic may still reference it (e.g., `if masks is not None and reproj_mask is not None:`), causing an `UnboundLocalError`. Pre-initializing ensures the variable exists and the conditional behaves correctly.
steps.side_effects: Changes control-flow behavior only in the `max_reproj_error is None` case: downstream mask-combination logic will now reliably treat `reproj_mask` as absent (`None`) instead of crashing. No expected impact on numerical results when `max_reproj_error` is provided, but any callers relying on the previous exception for signaling misconfiguration would no longer see that failure mode.

step_plan.summary: Fixes an `UnboundLocalError` in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap` by ensuring `reproj_mask` is defined even when `max_reproj_error` is `None`, preventing later conditional mask logic from referencing an uninitialized local.
